nodes.label: Graphify Skill
nodes.rationale: Turns any folder of code, docs, papers, images, or video into a persistent queryable knowledge graph with an honest EXTRACTED/INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS audit trail; when graph.json exists, questions go straight to query instead of rebuilding.

nodes.label: AST Structural Extraction
nodes.rationale: Deterministic and free: code is extracted via AST with no LLM and no API key, so a code-only corpus skips semantic extraction entirely.

nodes.label: Semantic Extraction Subagents
nodes.rationale: Docs/papers/images need LLM extraction; parallel general-purpose subagents dispatched in one message are 5-10x faster than reading files sequentially, and read-only Explore agents silently drop results because they cannot write chunk files.

nodes.label: Semantic Extraction Cache
nodes.rationale: Checked before dispatching subagents so unchanged files never re-pay LLM tokens; stale cache files are deleted so old results are never merged.

nodes.label: Community Detection
nodes.rationale: Surfaces cross-document connections you wouldn't think to ask about; communities are labeled with 2-5 word plain-language names after clustering.

nodes.label: Honesty Rules
nodes.rationale: Never invent an edge (use AMBIGUOUS if unsure), always show token cost, show raw cohesion scores, warn before visualizing >5000 nodes — keeps the graph a trustworthy audit trail rather than a hallucination.

nodes.label: Graph Health Check
nodes.rationale: Read-only integrity gate before labeling: surfaces edge collapse, dangling/missing endpoints, and self-loops — the silent-corruption modes of incremental updates and AST/LLM id mismatches. Never aborts, but warnings must be surfaced.

nodes.label: Shrink Guard (#479)
nodes.rationale: to_json refuses to write when the new graph is smaller than the existing graph.json, so an empty or failed extraction never clobbers a good graph; report and analysis sidecar are only written when the graph actually was.

nodes.label: Node ID Format Rule
nodes.rationale: IDs use the full repo-relative path stem plus entity so same-named files in different directories stay distinct and semantic IDs match AST-generated IDs; filename-only or parent-only stems create orphan ghost-duplicate nodes.

nodes.label: Confidence Score Rubric
nodes.rationale: Discrete values (0.95/0.85/0.75/0.65/0.55) instead of a continuous range because production showed models collapse range guidance to a bimodal binary (>50% at 0.5, >40% at 0.85+).

nodes.label: Constrained Query Expansion
nodes.rationale: The query CLI matches nodes via case-folded substring + IDF with no stemming, synonyms, or cross-language matching, so vocabulary mismatch collapses answers to noise; expanding the question against the graph's actual vocabulary (never inventing tokens) fixes this auditably.

nodes.label: BFS/DFS Traversal Modes
nodes.rationale: BFS (depth 3) for broad 'what is X connected to' context; DFS (depth-limited to 6) to trace a specific dependency chain. Output is token-budget aware, ranked by term relevance.

nodes.label: save-result Feedback Loop
nodes.rationale: Answers are saved back into the graph with useful/dead_end/corrected outcomes so the next --update extracts the Q&A as a node; graphify reflect distills these into LESSONS.md with preferred sources and known dead ends — a self-improving work memory.

nodes.label: Incremental Update (--update)
nodes.rationale: Re-extracts only new/changed files to save tokens and time; code-only changes skip semantic extraction entirely (no LLM needed), and deletions are pruned via an empty extraction merge.

nodes.label: build_merge Replace-on-Re-extract
nodes.rationale: Reads graph.json directly without a NetworkX round-trip so edge direction is preserved (#801); every source_file in new chunks is dropped from the base before merge so stale nodes don't survive, while prune_sources is reserved for genuinely deleted files (#1344, #1361).

nodes.label: Whisper Video Transcription
nodes.rationale: Video/audio cannot be read directly, so they are transcribed to text and treated as docs; a domain-hint initial prompt composed from god-node labels improves transcription accuracy with no extra API call.

nodes.label: URL Ingest (/graphify add)
nodes.rationale: Auto-detects URL type (YouTube, Twitter/X, arXiv, PDF, images, webpages) and saves to ./raw with author/contributor attribution, then merges into the graph via the update pipeline.

nodes.label: Watch Mode Auto-Rebuild
nodes.rationale: Code changes trigger immediate AST re-extraction with no LLM; doc/paper/image changes only write a needs_update flag because they require LLM re-extraction. Debounce waits until file activity stops so a wave of parallel agent writes triggers one rebuild, not one per file.

nodes.label: Graphify MCP Server
nodes.rationale: Stdio MCP server exposing query_graph, get_node, get_neighbors, get_community, god_nodes, graph_stats, shortest_path so other agents can query the graph live.

nodes.label: Graph Exports (Neo4j/FalkorDB/SVG/GraphML/Wiki)
nodes.rationale: Flag-gated exports; Cypher exports use MERGE so re-runs are idempotent, and FalkorDB prefers direct push because GRAPH.QUERY has no bulk script import.

nodes.label: Cross-Repo Graph Merge
nodes.rationale: Each repo/subfolder is extracted separately (the CLI places graphify-out inside the scanned path so parallel runs don't clobber a shared output dir), then merge-graphs combines them; each merged node carries a repo attribute for origin filtering.

nodes.label: Post-Commit Auto-Rebuild Hook
nodes.rationale: Triggers once per commit with no background process: detects changed code files via git diff HEAD~1 and re-runs AST extraction; appends to an existing hook rather than replacing it.

nodes.label: Native CLAUDE.md Integration
nodes.rationale: graphify claude install writes a '## graphify' section into the project CLAUDE.md so Claude checks the graph before answering codebase questions in every future session — no manual /graphify invocation needed.

nodes.label: Project CLAUDE.md graphify Rules
nodes.rationale: Instructs query-first workflow: graphify query/path/explain return a scoped subgraph much smaller than GRAPH_REPORT.md or raw grep output; run graphify update after code changes (AST-only, no API cost).

nodes.label: MedProj Product Vision
nodes.rationale: Turn physical therapy into something patients want to do; success = completed prescriptions, reduced dropout, and effort that has meaning beyond the patient. Reference feel is Forest app + Calm; anti-references are consumer-toy cuteness, legacy clinical UI, and aggressive gamification.

nodes.label: AI-Guided Exercise Sessions
nodes.rationale: The front camera reads posture in real time so patients practice correctly and safely without a human therapist present; the AI should feel like a calm silent guide, not a robotic overlay.

nodes.label: Tree Growth Metaphor
nodes.rationale: Sessions earn stars that grow a virtual tree; at a milestone a partner NGO plants a real tree in the patient's name — connecting every movement to something that grows, so the exercise is never just a rep count.

nodes.label: Gentle Accountability Principle
nodes.rationale: Progress shown with warmth, not pressure: missing a day makes the tree pause, not die. Duolingo's urgent-streak shame loop is explicitly the wrong model for recovering patients.

nodes.label: WCAG 2.1 AA Accessibility Target
nodes.rationale: Healthcare-adjacent users may have limited mobility: touch targets >=48px, camera-based detection must degrade gracefully when denied, reduced-motion support for vestibular sensitivities, and color never the sole indicator of feedback.

nodes.label: MedProj Design System
nodes.rationale: Mood: 'first morning in a greenhouse'. Restrained color for the light app shell, committed dark theme for the session screen. Outfit display + DM Sans body, 4px spacing base, 48px minimum touch targets.

nodes.label: Moss Green OKLCH Palette
nodes.rationale: Brand seed oklch(0.550 0.145 150) moss green on pure white — the green primary carries the nature feel so the background stays neutral; amber-gold accent reserved for stars and milestones.

nodes.label: Dark Session Mode
nodes.rationale: Full-screen deep forest dark theme for the camera + exercise screen: camera feed behind content, posture skeleton overlay in brighter session green, guidance text at bottom safe area, exit button >=48x48px.

nodes.label: Organic Motion System
nodes.rationale: Slow and organic, like watching a plant open. No bounce or elastic except --ease-grow's gentle overshoot reserved for tree growth (the signature animation); every animation needs a prefers-reduced-motion fallback and reveals must never gate content visibility.

nodes.label: Tree Progress Card
nodes.rationale: Central dashboard hero: full-width organic tree silhouette whose background shifts with growth stage; no card border or shadow — the tree lives directly on the page background.

nodes.label: Day Strip Component
nodes.rationale: 7-day horizontal week strip: done = primary green, missed = muted, future = border — status by color plus position.

nodes.label: Design Critique 2026-06-27 (17/40)
nodes.rationale: Verdict: a prototype masquerading as a product — tree metaphor and custom SVG show craft, but core flows are incomplete. Biggest opportunity: finish the session experience (pause, progress-saving, real-time feedback, celebration that doesn't auto-dismiss).

nodes.label: P0: No Session Pause/Cancel-with-Save
nodes.rationale: A user doing PT at home WILL be interrupted; losing all progress on exit is punishing in an injury-recovery context. Fix: pause button, exit prompt with save, countdown on resume.

nodes.label: P0: No First-Run Onboarding
nodes.rationale: First-time users land with zero context (what's a star? what exercise?) and the camera activates with no warning. Fix: 3-step dismissible overlay explaining tree/stars/camera.

nodes.label: P1: No Feedback During Rep Intervals
nodes.rationale: The 40-second session feels frozen with no indication the AI is processing between reps. Fix: skeleton overlay, real-time posture feedback, 'Analyzing...' indicator.

nodes.label: P1: Misleading Progress Calendar
nodes.rationale: Calendar shows mostly grey squares because it only tracks the last session, not full history. Fix: track an array of completed dates and render all as green.

nodes.label: User Authentication System
nodes.rationale: Supabase-backed login/signup with secure sessions and auto-redirect: logged-in users go to dashboard, logged-out users go to login.

nodes.label: Session Tracking
nodes.rationale: Session page saves duration, reps completed vs target, form quality score, and per-rep hold duration/form score to the database.

nodes.label: Progress Dashboard
nodes.rationale: Weekly stat cards, Recharts line chart of form quality over 14 days, bar chart of sessions per day, and this-week-vs-last-week improvement percentage.

nodes.label: Supabase Schema (profiles/therapy_sessions/rep_data)
nodes.rationale: Three tables defined in supabase/schema.sql with automatic profile creation on signup; all protected by Row Level Security policies.

nodes.label: Row Level Security
nodes.rationale: Users can only see/modify their own data; no direct table access — everything goes through Supabase policies.

nodes.label: Form Quality Score
nodes.rationale: Calculated as percentage of time in 'good' posture during a session — the quality proxy stored per session and per rep (100 = good, 50 = adjust, 0 = analyzing).

nodes.label: Legacy Warm Earthy Design
nodes.rationale: Describes warm cream backgrounds (#F7F4EF), terracotta accents (#C4612F), and Fraunces display serif — a design description that conflicts with the current moss-green DESIGN.md system, indicating an earlier design iteration.

nodes.label: Auth & Tracking Setup Guide
nodes.rationale: Step-by-step Supabase setup: create project, run schema.sql, copy API keys into .env.local (never committed), disable email confirmation for testing.

nodes.label: Dynamic Session Player (Phase 5)
nodes.rationale: Replaces the hardcoded Shoulder Raise validation with a database-driven generic engine — the final phase tying the admin pose system together: admins record (Phase 3), refine (Phase 4), and deploy (Phase 5) exercises with validation, feedback, hold durations, and instructions all driven by pose_criteria JSON.

nodes.label: analyzeExercise()
nodes.rationale: Generic validation: checks target body part visibility, validates angle criteria (min/max/target between three keypoints) and leveling rules (symmetry), returning ExerciseAnalysis with meetsAllCriteria, feedback, message, and failedCriteria.

nodes.label: GenericRepCounter
nodes.rationale: Works with any exercise type (static holds or dynamic movements) using a configurable holdThresholdMs from the database; counts a rep when the user enters the correct position, holds for the threshold, then exits.

nodes.label: pose_criteria JSON
nodes.rationale: Database-stored validation spec (targetBodyParts, angle criteria with relativeTo keypoints, leveling rules) so admins define new exercises without code changes — validation logic becomes data.

nodes.label: Exercises Table
nodes.rationale: Holds name, description, exercise_type (static/dynamic), pose_criteria, target_reps, hold_duration_ms, and feedback_messages — the session player fetches the first active exercise from it on mount.

nodes.label: Legacy Validation Backward Compatibility
nodes.rationale: analyzeShoulderRaise(), RepCounter, and shouldersInFrame() remain exported unchanged because the admin recording/editing pages (Phases 3 & 4) still depend on them.
